title: Quality-Attribute Architecture, ADRs, and Evolvable Interfaces · Changed-Stakeholder Lab
summary: An independent public-benefit team supplies different users, constraints, risks, technology, data, and failure conditions and transfers quality-attribute architecture, adrs, and evolvable interfaces into a changed quality-attribute architecture and interface suite. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of quality-attribute architecture and interface suite with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice…
objectives: Express accessibility, security, privacy, reliability, performance, modifiability, operability, and cost as stimulus-response-measure scenarios.
Compare modular monolith, service, event, batch, managed, and buy options against explicit quality scenarios and team constraints.
Design runtime-validated APIs or events with compatibility, versioning, idempotency, pagination, error, deprecation, and consumer evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-2 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-2
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Lab Quality Scenario
steps.instruction:
lab case — A team proposes five services without quality scenarios, independent deployment need, compatibility policy, failure containment, or ownership capacity. The lab variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: defend one bounded product claim

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Lab Quality Scenario.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for express accessibility, security, privacy, reliability, performance, modifiability, operability, and cost as stimulus-response-measure scenarios. For Commit to the mission model · Lab Quality Scenario, keep Quality Scenario connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: A team proposes five services without quality scenarios, independent deployment need, compatibility policy, failure containment, or ownership capacity. The lab variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: defend one bounded product claim

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Quality Scenario condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: NIST Secure Software Development Framework — Controls prepared organizations, protected software, well-secured production, vulnerability response, provenance, verification, and release evidence.

hints: For Commit to the mission model · Lab Quality Scenario, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Quality Scenario precondition, or invariant that must hold.

The lab variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: defend one bounded product claim

Words like scalable and secure are usable architecture requirements. That claim fails the commit to the mission model evidence boundary for Quality Scenario.

For commit to the mission model, apply Quality Scenario without its stated preconditions, omit the quality-attribute architecture and interface suite trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Adr Review Trigger

content.code:
pp2-adr-review-trigger-p2h2qd:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Adr Review Trigger structural check is only one gate. quality-attribute architecture and interface suite must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model · Lab Adr Review Trigger
steps.instruction:
Write the lab evidence note for Adr Review Trigger: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A team proposes five services without quality scenarios, independent deployment need, compatibility policy, failure containment, or ownership capacity. The lab variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. For Defend the transferred model · Lab Adr Review Trigger, keep Adr Review Trigger connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Attach evidence, consequence, owner, expiry, and measurable review triggers to architecture decisions.
